summary: Implemented and near-matched sysdolphin baselib spline text, adding spline point evaluation and arc-length parameterization routines while replacing placeholder spline declarations with a real HSD_Spline layout. The PR incorporated decomp.me matches for splGetSplinePoint at 100%, splArcLengthGetParameter at 98%, splArcLengthPoint at 100%, and splGetHelmite at 100% from prior work. It also propagated the corrected HSD_Spline* typing into ground and lbshadow call sites, removing s32 casts in grKongo and Ground_801C4B50.
what_changed: Added spline.c implementations for splGetSplinePoint, splArcLengthGetParameter, splArcLengthPoint, and helper inlines used by those routines.
Expanded struct HSD_Spline in spline.h from a placeholder u8 x0 UNK_SIZE_ARRAY to a concrete field layout: x0, x1, x2, x4, x8, xC, segments, and coeffs.
Corrected spline API prototypes so splGetSplinePoint takes Vec3*, HSD_Spline*, f32 and splArcLengthGetParameter takes HSD_Spline*, f32.
Changed Ground_801C4B50 signature from untyped s32 arguments to HSD_Spline*, Vec3*, Vec3*, f32 and updated its calls to splGetSplinePoint and lbShadow_8000E9F0 accordingly.
Updated grKongo_801D6668 to pass temp_r4->gv.kongo2.xC4 and Vec3 stack addresses directly instead of casting spline and Vec3 pointers through s32.
Updated lbShadow_8000E9F0 declaration to accept HSD_Spline* instead of s32.
Added necessary includes in spline.c, including placeholder.h, forward.h, math.h, and MetroTRK/intrinsics.h, and added baselib/forward.h in ground.c.
smart_moves: Used the newly understood HSD_Spline struct to remove unsafe integer-style pointer parameters and casts at downstream call sites.
Kept matching-sensitive helper logic as static or inline functions in spline.c, including separate splGetSplinePoint_inline and splGetSplinePoint_inline2 paths for spline mode handling.

Preserved comments around difficult matching in splArcLengthGetParameter, including the note that the math appears to use Simpson's rule and the PR body note that this function still required register swaps.
Handled endpoint behavior in splGetSplinePoint explicitly for arg2 == 1.0F with per-spline-type cases, which is important for reproducing original control flow.
Used sqrtf__Ff from MetroTRK intrinsics in splArcLengthPolynomial, indicating attention to the exact library call needed for matching.
decomp_lessons: When a library struct becomes understood, immediately propagate the real pointer type into callers and headers; doing so can eliminate misleading s32 casts and improve future decomp readability.
Spline code is highly sensitive to inline boundaries, switch structure, and temporary ordering; helper inlines may be useful both for readability and for achieving the original register allocation.
Arc-length parameter conversion for non-linear spline types can be represented as a binary search plus Simpson's rule integration over precomputed coefficients.
For near matches, preserve documented failed or partial inline attempts when they explain why remaining register swaps exist.

Header cleanup can be part of matching work: replacing placeholder includes and incomplete prototypes with forward declarations and real struct fields unlocks more precise typing across unrelated systems.
naming_conventions: Retained baselib spline function names with the spl prefix: splGetHelmite, splGetSplinePoint, splArcLengthGetParameter, splArcLengthPoint.
Named local helper routines with the source function prefix plus descriptive suffixes: splGetSplinePoint_inline, splGetSplinePoint_inline2, spl_GetCoeffs, spl_IterateSimpsonsMiddle.
HSD_Spline fields remained offset-style names x0, x1, x2, x4, x8, xC where semantic names were not yet evidenced.
Ground and lbshadow prototypes use project address-style comments such as /* 1C4B50 */ and /* 00E9F0 */ while improving parameter types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: splGetSplinePoint was reported as a 100% decomp.me match and split into inlines to reproduce mode-specific point calculations.
splArcLengthGetParameter was reported as 98% and explicitly noted by the author as requiring a decent amount of register swaps.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in splGetSplinePoint, suggesting stack padding was required for matching.
Included comments that different attempted inlines were left around, implying inline-shape experimentation was part of the matching strategy.
Used ABS(start - end) >= 0.00001F and Simpson integration helper calls in a loop to approximate the original control flow for arc-length inversion.
Used exact float constants such as 1.0F / 6.0F, 0.00001F, and -0.001F in polynomial and integration code, which are likely matching-sensitive.
review_feedback: No review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states that splArcLengthGetParameter still had register-swap issues and that comments about attempted inlines were intentionally left in the code.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish splArcLengthGetParameter from 98% to 100% or remove register swaps.
Search for additional HSD_Spline field naming improvements beyond x0, x1, x2, x4, x8, xC, segments, and coeffs.
Check implementation or callers of lbShadow_8000E9F0 to ensure its signature was later updated consistently in source, not just the header.
Search for further baselib spline matching work involving spline type cases 0, 1, 2, and 3.
